title: The verification safety net — teach stack-verify to target an OFFSET stack + scope to the services actually brought up, then auto-run it (NON-FATAL) at the bring-up tail so 'UP' means verified-working, not just containers-started. Owns the offset/project/scope awareness (the new lib/target.sh + the registry-recorded-port cross-check), the $DEVDIR->$STACK_ROOT bugfix, the cheap-win /api/health + sentinel.casbin_rules>0 asserts (the ISSUE-7 silent-403 catcher), the default-on+non-fatal auto-wire at both bring-up tails, and the net-new corpus/ops/verification.md contract.
position_note: M18 is the 3rd of v1.3b 'dress rehearsal' (M16->M20). It makes a bring-up self-verifying so the later M19 (frontend tier) + M20 (lifecycle convergence / auto-chaining) inherit a working stack rather than a started-but-maybe-broken one. Depends on M16 (clean baseline) + M12's unified registry (recorded ports). Parallel-with M17 (different surfaces).
kind: a code milestone (offset/scope-aware stack-verify + the bring-up-tail auto-wire) + one net-new corpus doc. ALL testable logic lives in the SEPARATE nested .agentspace/rosetta-extensions repo (bash: stack-verify lib/target.sh + lib/services.sh + lib/readiness.sh + live/verify.sh + live/autoverify.sh + repos/run.sh + census/inventory.sh, bring-up wiring demo-stack/up-injected.sh + dev-stack/dev-stack; python: the net-new stack-verify/tests/test_verify.py). The rosetta m18/verify-net branch carries only corpus/ops/verification.md (net-new) + the index rows (ops README + CLAUDE.md) + the rosetta_demo.md cross-link + the milestone tracking docs.

tests.language: python (the net-new stack-verify/tests/test_verify.py — stdlib unittest, driving the bash via subprocess) + shellcheck/py_compile on the 9 touched scripts + markdown (the net-new verification.md = the rosetta deliverable). No Go touched.
tests.go_note: Unchanged by M18 — M18 touched only bash + Python. All 4 Go modules still build + pass -count=1; gofmt + go vet clean (regression-checked at close, not re-counted).
tests.python_note: +82 collected — the net-new stack-verify/tests/test_verify.py (stack-verify had NO Python tests before, only Playwright e2e). 18 test classes covering: TestOffsetAwareness (offset-resolved ports/containers), TestServiceScopeFilter + TestReadinessScopeFilter (BOTH liveness AND readiness honour STACK_SERVICES — the readiness-phase scope-filter bug found+fixed in harden Pass 1), TestTargetHelpers/Boundaries (the pure parse+resolve helpers incl. the close FINDING-A1 non-numeric-offset sanitize), TestCrossCheck/Edges (the base-port-band registry cross-check, never the broken decade lane), TestOffsetMatrixSweep (every base x N, roadrunner's high base), TestDevdirBugfix + TestRunShResolution + TestInventoryShResolution ($STACK_ROOT fix, static+behavioral), TestAutoVerify/Edges (non-fatal failure modes — always exit 0), TestProbeService (the per-service liveness classifier), TestContainerForProject, TestBringUpWiring (both tails default-on+non-fatal), TestShellcheck, TestAutoVerifyCrossCheckIntegration. Trajectory: build 32 -> harden 79 (+47, incl. the readiness-scope-filter fix) -> close 82 (+3, the FINDING-A1 regression).
tests.shellcheck: all 9 M18-touched scripts shellcheck-CLEAN (-S warning): stack-verify lib/target.sh, lib/services.sh, lib/readiness.sh, live/verify.sh, live/autoverify.sh, repos/run.sh, census/inventory.sh + bring-up demo-stack/up-injected.sh + dev-stack/dev-stack.
tests.flake_gate: 5/5 clean, sequential, on the milestone-touched suite (stack-verify/tests/test_verify.py, 82 tests). Deterministic — 82 passed on every run. 0 flakes.

coverage.note: Bash tooling is path-enumerated via the Python harness (no kcov; matches M16/M17 bash-tooling practice — coverage as candidate-finder, not a numeric gate). Harden ran 3 passes (recorded in progress.md); the loop stopped after Pass 3 found no new seam worth pinning.
coverage.seams_pinned: every milestone-touched seam: the full offset matrix (every base x N), BOTH-phase scope filter (liveness + readiness), the cross-check edges (single-in-band-among-junk / all-out-of-band / empty / malformed-JSON / missing-file / roadrunner-only), the target-helper boundaries (empty/non-numeric N, demo-0, multi-dash, exact-match skill!=skiller, ragged whitespace, non-numeric offset), the autoverify non-fatal failure modes, probe_service's 4 probe kinds x status branches, container_for_project's anchored prefix swap, the run.sh + inventory.sh $STACK_ROOT resolution (static + behavioral), and the bring-up wiring.

non_fatal_invariant_verification.verdict: GREEN — the auto-verify can NEVER abort a genuinely good bring-up, and does NOT false-down a healthy offset stack or a reduced-profile bring-up.
non_fatal_invariant_verification.always_exits_0: autoverify.sh is deliberately NOT `set -e` (set -uo pipefail only); every internal probe is wrapped (|| true / || echo 0 / captured rc); the script's final statement is `exit 0`. Both call sites (up-injected.sh:90, dev-stack:132) ALSO append `|| true` — two independent non-fatality guarantees. Pinned by TestAutoVerify + TestAutoVerifyEdges (every failure mode -> exit 0).
non_fatal_invariant_verification.offset_from_recorded_ports: the offset is the N the bring-up already KNOWS (passed explicitly via --offset), cross-checked NON-FATALLY against the registry's RECORDED host ports via a base-port BAND [3000,11000] (target_cross_check) — never a drift-prone bare formula, and never the broken `port//10000==n` decade lane (which would false-warn roadrunner's high base). Pinned by TestCrossCheck/Edges + TestOffsetMatrixSweep.
non_fatal_invariant_verification.both_phases_scope_filter: the harden Pass-1 bug (the readiness phase ran 6 deep probes UNCONDITIONALLY while only liveness honoured STACK_SERVICES -> a reduced bring-up false-downed graphql/gotenberg/sentinel/storage) is FIXED (commit 2f412a3): run_readiness threads the backing service name + gates on target_service_selected, the same gate as service_rows. Verified held at close (TestReadinessScopeFilter green; the doc verification.md states both phases honour the filter).
non_fatal_invariant_verification.close_finding_a1: adversarial Phase 2c found a residual robustness gap: a non-numeric --offset/STACK_OFFSET flowed verbatim into 3 `$(( base + offset ))` sites -> 'unbound variable' under set -u, silently SKIPPING the cheap-win asserts (the invariant still held — exit 0 — but the asserts vanished with a confusing message). Fixed at the single resolution boundary (target_resolve_offset validates ^[0-9]+$ else warns non-fatally + derives, M18-D7). Not a production path (real tails always pass numeric offsets); hardens operator/test-platform typo paths. Regression pinned at unit + integration level.

delivered.verification_md: corpus/ops/verification.md (net-new, ~150 lines, dual-level + code-cited): the one-paragraph contract (default-on + always-exit-0), the offset/scope model table (STACK_PROJECT/STACK_OFFSET/STACK_SERVICES), the correctness mitigation (derive-from-known + registry cross-check + non-fatal-always, with #M18-D1/D3/D5 back-ref tags), the cheap-win ISSUE-7-catcher asserts, what runs on which ports (both phases honour the scope filter), the $DEVDIR->$STACK_ROOT fix, and how to read a warning block. Indexed in ops README + root CLAUDE.md; cross-linked from rosetta_demo.md, bidirectional with idempotency.md/safety.md/test-platform.
delivered.target_sh: stack-verify/lib/target.sh (net-new resolution helper): target_project_n (parse <type>-<N>), target_resolve_offset (honour explicit, else derive, NOW sanitizing non-numeric input — #M18-D2/D7), target_cross_check (base-port-band registry cross-check, non-fatal warn — #M18-D1/D5), target_service_selected (exact-match scope filter — #M18-D2). Sourced by services.sh + readiness.sh.
delivered.offset_scope_aware_probes: services.sh::service_rows + readiness.sh now apply the offset to host ports + rewrite the container project prefix (anthropos-cms-1 -> demo-3-cms-1) + honour STACK_SERVICES in BOTH phases. The SERVICES table stays a single base-port source of truth; the offset is applied once, centrally.
delivered.autoverify: stack-verify/live/autoverify.sh (net-new, NON-FATAL wrapper — #M18-D3): the cheap-win asserts (/api/health on 8082+offset + sentinel.casbin_rules>0 via docker exec, gated by the scope filter — #M18-D4) then the scoped verify live, always exit 0, loud warn + /test-platform hint on failure.
delivered.devdir_fix: repos/run.sh + census/inventory.sh: $DEVDIR (UNDEFINED) -> $STACK_ROOT/$repo (repos are siblings of platform/). The old bug collapsed every repo to /$repo -> 'not cloned' on the first run.
delivered.bring_up_wire: the auto-verify wired default-on + non-fatal at both tails: demo-stack/up-injected.sh (--project demo-N --offset N*10000, opt-out DEMO_NO_VERIFY) + dev-stack/dev-stack cmd_up (--project dev-n --offset n*10000, opt-out DEV_NO_VERIFY) — mirrors dev-setdress's proven pattern.

review.findings_note: 3 findings — FINDING-A1 (code-quality must-fix, from adversarial Phase 2c): a non-numeric offset crashed 3 arithmetic sites under set -u, silently skipping the cheap-win asserts (non-fatal held); fixed at target_resolve_offset + 3 regression tests + M18-D7. A test gap (the A1 regression) — landed. A decision-triage item — M18-D1/D3/D5 reference tags added to verification.md (substance was already present). No scope finding (all 6 deliverables Fate-1).
review.scope: GREEN — all 6 deliverables + 4 verification items checked; every overview Scope.In item delivered Fate-1; the Out items (frontend-port verification -> M19 where the frontends first exist, deep e2e -> /test-platform) stay out — a clean scope boundary, not a deferral. 0 orphan TODO/FIXME in either repo.
review.code_quality: GREEN after the A1 fix — autoverify is structurally non-fatal (no set -e, every probe wrapped, always exit 0, call sites || true too); the offset cross-check uses the base-port band not the broken decade lane; both phases honour the scope filter; the SERVICES table stays one base-port source of truth, offset applied centrally; container prefix swap is anchored (only leading anthropos-). shellcheck (9 scripts) + py_compile clean. 0 remaining must-fix.
review.docs: GREEN — verification.md accurate vs code (verified the non-fatal contract, the offset table, the both-phase scope filter, the cheap-win asserts, the $STACK_ROOT fix against source), dual-level, indexed in ops README + CLAUDE.md, cross-linked bidirectionally; all outbound links resolve. Per-unit handbook contract: verification.md IS the new unit's doc, indexed. README quotes no test count -> no count-drift to reconcile.
review.tests: GREEN — the full stack-verify suite 82/82 (run, not a touched-subset); all 4 Go modules build + pass -count=1 (unchanged from M17). Python 191->273 collected (+82, the net-new suite). Flake gate 5/5 deterministic. Every seam pinned; the A1 fix has unit + integration regression.
review.decision_triage: M18-D1 (derive+cross-check), D3 (non-fatal contract), D5 (base-port band) blended into verification.md with (#M18-DK) back-ref tags. D2 (the target.sh env contract), D4 (cheap-win inside autoverify), D6 (new test file), D7 (the A1 sanitize) -> archived in decisions.md (implementation-mechanism / maintainer-only).
review.adversarial: Phase 2c — 4 scenarios against the non-fatal invariant: no-registry/no-docker/reduced-scope (exit 0 OK), empty --services under set -u (exit 0 OK), no --project (skip+exit 0 OK), non-numeric --offset (FOUND FINDING-A1 — crashed the arithmetic but still exit 0; fixed). The invariant held in EVERY scenario (the bring-up was never abortable); A1 was a clarity/robustness gap on a non-production input path. Scenarios recorded in decisions.md.
review.deferral_audit: GREEN — knowledge/plan/releases/01.3b-dress-rehearsal/m18-verify-net/audit-deferrals/deferral-audit-2026-06-09-m18-close.md. M18 added 0 new deferrals (every deliverable + D1-D7 a Fate-1 landing). The only open item is the inherited cross-release DEF-M10-01 (S3 blobs + cloud store -> v1.4, signed) — untouched by M18 (M18 touched verify, NOT the snapshot-store/S3 area), all 4 aging triggers negative, authority intact. 0 repeat, 0 chronic, 0 aged-out.

notes: M18 is the 3rd milestone of v1.3b 'dress rehearsal' — it makes a bring-up self-verifying so 'UP' carries a real promise (the ISSUE-7 silent-403 stack would now be caught at bring-up time). The defining constraint was the LOAD-BEARING NON-FATAL INVARIANT: the auto-verify must never block a genuinely good bring-up and must not false-down a healthy offset stack or a reduced-profile bring-up. The close verified this holds on three axes (always-exit-0 wrapper + || true call sites; offset derived-from-known and cross-checked against recorded ports via a base-port band not a drift-prone formula; BOTH liveness and readiness honour the scope filter — the harden Pass-1 readiness-filter bug fix held). Adversarial Phase 2c found one residual robustness gap (FINDING-A1: a non-numeric offset crashed the arithmetic under set -u and silently skipped the cheap-win asserts — the invariant still held but the asserts vanished); fixed at the single resolution boundary (M18-D7) with unit+integration regression. Reconciled the dress-rehearsal-m18 tag from the build HEAD 594b9cf to the final HEAD 777723a (the tag trailed the 3 harden commits + the close fix), re-consuming stack-demo to it. Deferral re-audit GREEN. Go 736 unchanged, Python 191->273 collected (+82, the net-new stack-verify suite), flake 0 (5/5). Next: M19 (the frontend tier).
